personas.subtitle: Format-matched control condition
personas.when_to_use: Use as the format-matched control for prompted-persona comparisons.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to absorb prompt-presence effects without steering answers toward a specific reasoning posture.

personas.subtitle: Strongest-objection stress test
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to challenge their own answer before committing.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to widen the distribution by increasing objections, caveats, or reversals.

personas.subtitle: Problem decomposition before synthesis
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to break complex questions into parts before answering.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to increase structured, decomposition-led answers and reduce leaps to conclusion.

personas.subtitle: Multiple candidate answers before narrowing
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want breadth before commitment on ambiguous questions.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to widen the distribution by surfacing more alternative framings or candidate conclusions.

personas.subtitle: Single-best-answer commitment
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to converge on one answer under stated assumptions.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to tighten the distribution around clearer top-choice outputs.
